description: RAG Implementation with LangChain for WhatsApp Chatbot - Adds a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline built on LangChain that ingests Markdown docs, retrieves relevant chunks, generates grounded answers, extracts lead insights, and hands off to humans when confidence is low.

userStories.title: Define ingestion pipeline shape and config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single config module that centralizes the embedding model, chunking strategy, and retrieval parameters so the rest of the RAG code reads them from one place.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/config/rag.ts exports typed constants: EMBEDDING_MODEL = "text-embedding-3-small", EMBEDDING_DIMS = 1536, CHUNK_SIZE = 800, CHUNK_OVERLAP = 120, RETRIEVER_K = 4, SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.72
Reads OPENAI_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from process.env, fails fast with a clear error at boot if either is missing
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-21. Created src/config/rag.ts with typed constants and fail-fast secret accessors (getOpenAIApiKey, getAnthropicApiKey, assertRagSecrets).

userStories.title: Extend KnowledgeDocument with ingestion-tracking fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to track the source file, chunk index, and content hash per row so re-ingestion can skip unchanged chunks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to KnowledgeDocument: sourcePath String, chunkIndex Int, contentHash String, createdAt DateTime @default(now()), updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
Add compound unique index on (sourcePath, chunkIndex) so upserts are deterministic
Add index on contentHash for fast skip lookups
Prisma migration generated and applied against a local DB (npx prisma migrate dev --name add_rag_ingestion_tracking)
npx prisma generate runs cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-21. Added sourcePath, chunkIndex, contentHash, createdAt, updatedAt to KnowledgeDocument in prisma/schema.prisma with compound unique index on (sourcePath, chunkIndex) and index on contentHash. Migration 20260422022358_add_rag_ingestion_tracking generated via `prisma migrate diff` (non-interactive workaround for migrate dev) and applied cleanly to local DB via `prisma migrate deploy`. prisma generate and typecheck pass; 43 tests green.

userStories.title: Build the ingestion CLI script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want npm run ingest:knowledge to walk documentation/, chunk every .md file, embed new/changed chunks, and upsert them into KnowledgeDocument.
acceptanceCriteria: New script src/scripts/ingestKnowledge.ts reads all *.md files under documentation/ recursively
Splits each file with LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter using CHUNK_SIZE / CHUNK_OVERLAP from src/config/rag.ts
Computes sha256(content) per chunk as contentHash
Upserts by (sourcePath, chunkIndex): insert if missing, skip if contentHash matches, update if differs
Deletes rows whose (sourcePath, chunkIndex) no longer exists in the file
Writes embeddings via raw SQL ($executeRaw) with ::vector casts
Logs a summary: N files, X inserted, Y updated, Z skipped, W deleted
New npm script: "ingest:knowledge": "ts-node src/scripts/ingestKnowledge.ts"
Running the script twice produces 0 inserted, 0 updated, N skipped on second run
Unit test covers the hash-skip logic with mocked embeddings + mocked Prisma client
Typecheck passes; tests pass (npm test)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-21. Created src/scripts/ingestKnowledge.ts with pure `runIngestion(deps)` core + thin CLI wrapper, using RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter from @langchain/textsplitters (new dep) with CHUNK_SIZE/CHUNK_OVERLAP from src/config/rag.ts. sha256 content hash per chunk; upsert-by-(source_path, chunk_index) with skip-if-hash-matches; delete rows whose keys no longer exist. Embeddings written via $executeRawUnsafe with ::vector cast. Added `ingest:knowledge` npm script. Unit test (tests/ingestKnowledge.test.ts) covers insert-all, skip-on-match, update-on-hash-delta, delete-stale, and mixed paths with mocked embedder + mocked Prisma; 50 tests green. Also fixed pre-existing typecheck error in src/scripts/seed-knowledge.ts by pointing import at @langchain/textsplitters.

userStories.title: Implement the pgvector retriever
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a LangChain VectorStoreRetriever that queries KnowledgeDocument by cosine similarity and returns the top K chunks plus their similarity scores.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/services/ragRetrieverService.ts exports retrieveRelevantChunks(query: string): Promise<RetrievedChunk[]> where RetrievedChunk = { id, content, title, score }
Generates the query embedding with the same EMBEDDING_MODEL used for ingestion
Runs a single raw SQL query using the pgvector cosine operator (<=> for distance or 1 - (embedding <=> $1) for similarity), ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 ASC LIMIT K
Rows with embedding IS NULL are excluded
Unit test with a seeded in-memory fixture (or mocked Prisma) verifies ordering and K limit
Typecheck passes; tests pass

userStories.title: Build the conversational RAG chain
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single function that, given a user message and a ChatSession.id, produces a grounded Portuguese reply.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/services/ragChainService.ts exports generateAnswer(input: { sessionId: string; userMessage: string })
Loads the last 10 Messages for the session (ordered by timestamp ASC) and formats them as LangChain HumanMessage / AIMessage history
Calls retrieveRelevantChunks(userMessage) and concatenates chunk text into a context string
If no chunk exceeds SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD, returns { answer: <fallback>, handoff: true } without calling the LLM
Builds a Portuguese system prompt encoding tone rules from documentation/buisness_plan.md section 6
Calls Claude Sonnet 4.6 via @langchain/anthropic with temperature: 0.2
Returns { answer: string, handoff: boolean, topScore: number, usedChunkIds: string[] }
Unit test with mocked LLM + mocked retriever verifies low-score handoff path, high-score LLM path, and that chat history is included in the prompt
Typecheck passes; tests pass

userStories.title: Wire the RAG chain into whatsappWorker.ts
userStories.description: As a user on WhatsApp, when I send a message the bot should reply with a grounded answer and persist both sides of the conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: whatsappWorker.ts job handler: finds-or-creates User by phoneNumber
Finds active ChatSession (status ACTIVE_BOT) or creates one
If session status is WAITING_HUMAN or RESOLVED, persist inbound Message and return (no auto-reply)
Persists inbound Message with senderType: TENANT
Calls generateAnswer({ sessionId, userMessage })
Persists outbound Message with senderType: BOT
If handoff is true, updates ChatSession.status to WAITING_HUMAN
Sends answer via existing whatsappService outbound call
Errors from RAG chain are caught; worker sends generic Portuguese apology and flips session to WAITING_HUMAN
Integration-style unit test with mocked Prisma + whatsappService + generateAnswer covers happy path, handoff path, and already-WAITING_HUMAN path
Typecheck passes; tests pass

userStories.title: Extract structured lead insights
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the chatbot to capture the tenant's rental preferences (budget, neighborhood, bedrooms, pets, intent) into the database so a human can follow up with real listings.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/services/leadExtractionService.ts exports extractInsights(input: { sessionId: string; userMessage: string }): Promise<ExtractedInsights>
Uses LangChain structured output with a Zod schema: { budget?, neighborhood?, bedrooms?, pets_allowed?, intent: "search"|"schedule_visit"|"contract_question"|"human_handoff"|"other" }
Runs against Claude Sonnet 4.6; temperature: 0
Invoked from whatsappWorker.ts AFTER the reply is sent (queueMicrotask or separate BullMQ job)
Finds-or-creates a RentalProcess for the tenant with status TRIAGE (one open process per tenant)
For each non-null extracted key, upserts an AiExtractedInsight row; updates existing rows rather than duplicating
If intent === "human_handoff", flips ChatSession to WAITING_HUMAN
Unit test with mocked LLM verifies insights are parsed, RentalProcess created once per tenant, insights upserted (second call with new budget updates, doesn't insert new row)
Typecheck passes; tests pass

userStories.title: Seed script for smoke testing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an evaluation fixture so I can spot-check RAG quality without touching WhatsApp.
acceptanceCriteria: New script src/scripts/evalRag.ts reads a hard-coded array of ~8 Portuguese test questions (triagem, visita, documentação, rescisão, pagamento)
For each question, calls generateAnswer with a throwaway ChatSession and prints: question, top chunk title, similarity score, handoff flag, final answer
New npm script "eval:rag": "ts-node src/scripts/evalRag.ts"
Typecheck passes
